text: I arrive as junior developer learning where to start. My past: I know Git basics but product menus and enterprise language can make me feel like I do not belong yet.
Today I want to: Find a clear beginner path without drowning in product categories.
My life goal is: Become employable without losing confidence to confusing tooling.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub

text: I see "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub".
I think: I landed on GitHub, but I do not yet see the beginner path I need.
My reaction: The headline feels confident, but as a junior developer I still feel outside the door.
Idea: show a clear beginner route like Learn Git, Make First Project, Build Portfolio before the bigger product choices.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life on one more step is worth it if it reveals a practical starting path toward becoming employable.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that GitHub matters for my goal, but this first view does not yet lower my confusion. Confidence: medium.
